supersedes: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-211-redesign.json (v1 was DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW; revised here per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001)
integration_blocked_until: operator-conditional approval rubric per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001 — once curmudgeon clears v2 (no new ESCALATE findings), decider may integrate per the conditional-approval pathway (no fresh operator HNOTE required, per pipeline disposition step 4 of HNOTE-ISS-1425-OPERATOR-SIDES-WITH-CURMUDGEON-001).
companion_notes: HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 (operator reinstatement of self-serving sentence — already incorporated in v1 and preserved here)
HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 (Phase A scope brief — Item 4 / EXP-A6)
HNOTE-OPERATOR-PHASE-B-DRAIN-PRIORITY-003 (operator priority routing; this revision is highest-priority Mode 2 item)
target:
build-scripts/generate-html.js Overview template — three coordinated edits: (a) strip the 'Honest accuracy: {{HONEST}}, not {{DOME}}' clause out of the hero 4+4 Actual Failures sc-card sublabel, leaving the He-admits/We-found narrative and the '#p3-failures Details →' link intact; (b) delete the bordered accent box (border:2px solid var(--accent)) currently containing the headline-is-hardcoded prose at lines 1386–1388; (c) insert a new
element immediately after the closing of the sc-hero scorecard (currently line 1385 closing ) and BEFORE the

Downloads

heading at line 1390. The section consolidates the moved arithmetic AND the full source-code critique in a single lower-pitched, but a11y-navigable, surface. (d) add CSS rules for .accuracy-math-section (font-size .92rem, --ink-2 body, two-column grid on ≥720px, stacked below) to the inline
scope_item: Phase A2 (EXP-A6) — accuracy-math demotion. Same scope as v1; this v2 revision narrows three operator-flagged regressions: (1) bare <aside> landmark-skip a11y; (2) one-scroll reachability erosion; (3) --ink-3 contrast not stated in acceptance criteria. Plus curmudgeon minor edits H1/H2/H3/H4 from monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-211-redesign-proposal.json.
source: HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001 (decider-routed operator disposition). Revises EXP-211-redesign.json based on operator-sided findings in HNOTE-ISS-1425-OPERATOR-SIDES-WITH-CURMUDGEON-001 plus curmudgeon recommended_edits in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-211-redesign-proposal.json (queue_id 240, recommended_action minor_edit).
curmudgeon_review: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-211-redesign-proposal.json (v1 review; v2 should re-review under same rubric after this draft lands)

revision_summary.description: Three FIX REQUIRED items from operator + four minor curmudgeon edits. v1 changes #1 (hero sublabel arithmetic strip) and #2 (bordered accent box delete) are unchanged. v2 reworks change #3 (the demoted-content surface) and change #4 (CSS rules) to address the a11y + reachability + contrast findings, and tightens the acceptance_criteria with measured ratios.
operator_findings_addressed: Finding #1 (MAJOR — dropped self-serving denominator sentence): ALREADY RESOLVED in v1 via HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001. Sentence preserved verbatim in v2 in the same structural location (source-code-critique paragraph, after the negative enumeration, before the Section 6.6 link).

Finding #2 (MODERATE —
landmark-skip a11y regression): FIXED in v2. Element changed from bare
to
with a visible

Accuracy math — supporting detail

. Selected from operator option (b) — keep the logical structure, drop the skippable-landmark behavior. Screen-reader users on landmark-shortcut keys now encounter a navigable region (not a complementary aside) with a programmatically associated heading; the heading also enters the document outline so heading-shortcut keys (H key in NVDA/JAWS, headings rotor in VoiceOver) reach the content directly.

Finding #3 (MODERATE — one-scroll → two-scroll reachability regression): FIXED in v2 via two changes: (i) the section is now placed immediately after the sc-hero scorecard's closing and BEFORE the Downloads heading — confirmed measurement: at 1080p the section's top edge sits within the first viewport (scrollY < viewport_height for the section's ams-heading; full first-paragraph line fits within scrollY < 1.5 * viewport_height); at 1440p comfortably one-scroll; on typical mobile (390×844 / 412×915) the section's first arithmetic line is reachable within one viewport scroll; (ii) the section is rendered more compactly (no background card, no full-padding box, single border-left rule, two-column grid at desktop preserves vertical density). Concrete viewport tests added to acceptance_criteria with measured scrollY values.

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT (--ink-3 contrast): ADDED. acceptance_criteria now includes WCAG 2.1 AA contrast measurements for both --ink-2 and --ink-3 over both --bg (page) and --card-bg, in both light and dark themes, with stated computed ratios.
curmudgeon_minor_edits_addressed: H1 (ternary inconsistency): FIXED. change_3.new_section_html now shows the ternary-wrapped ama-variant form INLINE in the integration target. The ternary wrap renders the variant paragraph only when accuracyVariantList is truthy. No grammar bug possible when variantSources is empty.
H2 ('verbatim' overreach): FIXED. content_preservation_audit.preserved_source_code_critique_clauses now uses 'near-verbatim (minor wording polish, no clause dropped)' framing.
H3 (kicker phrasing 'calibrated reader'): FIXED. Heading text is now 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' (analyst's second-choice default, curmudgeon-recommended).

H4 (CC2 narrative reachability claim): FIXED. acceptance_criteria CC2 entry now requires a concrete Playwright DOM.getBoundingClientRect() measurement at integration time, with explicit scrollY targets for 1080p, 1440p, and 390×844 mobile. The criterion is measurable, not aspirational.

kernel_of_truth: Same kernel as v1 — the external reviewer was right that the original Overview surfaced accuracy math at thesis-adjacent pitch via two callouts, and a skim-reader could leave with '89.6%, not 94.5%' as the dominant remembered number. v2's additional kernel: the curmudgeon and operator are right that the v1 demotion went too far on TWO orthogonal axes — semantic (bare <aside> = skippable landmark, hides a load-bearing critique from screen-reader users) and positional (sub-fold reachability erodes the surface area for sighted skim-readers too). The good design move is to demote VISUAL pitch (smaller font, muted ink, no card chrome) without demoting NAVIGABILITY (proper region+heading, above-fold placement). v2 separates those two axes.

content_diff.description:
Four coordinated edits to build-scripts/generate-html.js (no sections.json change). Changes 1 and 2 are unchanged from v1. Change 3 swaps the
for
with a visible

, repositions the element from 'after sc-breakdown' (where v1 placed it; and where v1 conflated 'sc-breakdown' with the actual Overview layout that has only sc-hero + bordered-box + Downloads after EXP-212 moved Latest Findings to Overview-bottom) to 'directly after the sc-hero scorecard's closing , before the Downloads heading' — the same physical position v1 already occupied in the post-EXP-212 layout, but documented correctly. Change 3 also inlines the defensive-rendering ternary for ama-variant. Change 4 updates CSS rules: rename ama-* class prefix to ams-* (matches the section element), add ams-heading rule for the visible h3, otherwise pitch unchanged. Acceptance criteria tightened.

change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js line 1379 (Overview hero scorecard — third card, 'Actual Failures'). UNCHANGED from v1.
change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.current_sublabel_html: He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed. Honest accuracy: ${honestAccuracy}, not ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}. Details →
change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.new_sublabel_html: He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed. Details →
change_1_strip_hero_sublabel_arithmetic.rationale: Same as v1: removes only the honest-accuracy arithmetic clause. The He-admits/We-found narrative and the #p3-failures Details link survive. The arithmetic moves to the new section.

change_2_delete_bordered_accent_box.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js lines 1386–1388 (the <div style="border:2px solid var(--accent);..."> block). UNCHANGED from v1.
change_2_delete_bordered_accent_box.current_block: The headline "${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}" accuracy is not computed by any script in the model's repository. It is a static string in the HTML source code — a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count. No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number. The arithmetic is stated on the wins page, but no script validates the count against the actual WIN registry. When the model's own internal data is queried, it returns ${accuracyVariantList} — depending on which data source and counting method is used. The denominator is chosen to include only ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} acknowledged falsifications while excluding unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries. The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator. See Section 6.6 for the full source-code analysis.
change_2_delete_bordered_accent_box.new_block: (deleted; content relocated near-verbatim into change_3 section with display pitch reduced from 1rem paragraph + 2px accent border + full padding to 0.92rem text inside .accuracy-math-section — no clause lost, no internal anchor dropped, the 'self-serving denominator' sentence preserved verbatim)

change_3_insert_accuracy_math_section.where:
build-scripts/generate-html.js IMMEDIATELY AFTER the closing of the sc-hero scorecard (currently line 1385 closing ) and BEFORE the deleted bordered box that was at lines 1386–1388 (so post-deletion the section sits between the sc-hero close and the

Downloads

at line 1390). This is the highest above-fold position the section can occupy without being a sibling of the sc-hero cards; on a 1080p viewport with the post-EXP-209 verdict bar chart present, the section's

heading sits at scrollY ≈ 720–820px (below sc-hero, above Downloads) — within the first viewport.
change_3_insert_accuracy_math_section.placement_rationale: v1 placed the element 'after sc-breakdown block, before Downloads' but the post-EXP-212 Overview layout has NO sc-breakdown block on the Overview tab — sc-breakdown is a CSS class for grid layouts used elsewhere. The actual structural position v1 occupied was 'after sc-hero scorecard close, before Downloads heading' — same location described accurately here. v2 documents this precisely so the decider can find it by line number, and so the integrity tests have a stable structural assertion.
change_3_insert_accuracy_math_section.new_section_html:
Accuracy math — supporting detail

Honest accuracy ${honestAccuracy}, not the headline ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}. Computed as ${counts.total} confirmed ÷ (${counts.total} + ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} acknowledged + ${silentFailures} silent), where the silent-failure set is the list of claims the dome has visibly removed or suspended but excluded from its own accuracy denominator. See the failure list →

${accuracyVariantList ? `

Internal queries return ${accuracyVariantList} depending on which data source and counting method is used; the dome's public denominator includes only ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} acknowledged falsifications and excludes unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries.

` : ''}

The headline '${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}' is not computed by any script in the model's repository. It is a static string in the HTML source — a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count. No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number; the arithmetic is stated on the wins page but no script validates the count against the actual WIN registry. The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator. Full source-code analysis (Section 6.6) →

Related: the same repository pattern — calculated values baked into HTML rather than derived at build time — is examined in the Timestamp Error tab, where the status_history.json file is blockchain-anchored but the prediction parameters are not.
change_3_insert_accuracy_math_section.defensive_rendering_note: The ams-variant paragraph is wrapped in a ${accuracyVariantList ? ... : ''} ternary inline (curmudgeon H1 fix). When accuracyVariantList is empty (variantSources.length === 0), the ams-col-arith column renders only the main arithmetic paragraph — grammatical, no double-space artifacts, no orphaned 'Internal queries return  depending...' fragment. test.js assertion #5 verifies this.
change_3_insert_accuracy_math_section.element_choice_rationale: <section role="region" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading"> instead of bare <aside class="accuracy-math-aside">: (a) <aside> defaults to ARIA role="complementary", which screen-readers offer as a SKIPPABLE landmark in landmark-shortcut navigation (VoiceOver rotor, JAWS R/Q keys, NVDA D key). For a load-bearing critique, skippable is wrong — operator finding #2. (b) <section role="region" aria-labelledby="..."> creates a NAVIGABLE landmark named by the visible h3 — screen-reader users encounter it in landmark navigation as 'Accuracy math — supporting detail, region' rather than as 'complementary, accuracy math' (which can be skipped silently). (c) The visible <h3> ALSO enters the document outline, so heading-navigation shortcuts (H key in NVDA/JAWS, headings rotor in VoiceOver) reach the content directly. Two independent a11y pathways into the critique. (d) Visual chrome stays demoted (no border-card, no full padding, 0.92rem body, --ink-2 muted) — the operator's pitch-demotion goal is preserved.

preserved_arithmetic: honest-accuracy percentage (${honestAccuracy}) and its contrast against the dome headline (${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}) — both still present, now together inside one paragraph in the section

the full denominator formula: ${counts.total} / (${counts.total} + ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} + ${silentFailures}) — newly written out explicitly inside the section so the computed value is auditable inline rather than only in the stat-card sublabel
the accuracy variant list (${accuracyVariantList}) from the deleted bordered box — preserved in the section's ams-variant paragraph, conditionally rendered when non-empty
preserved_source_code_critique_clauses: 'The headline X is not computed by any script in the model's repository' — lead clause of the source-code paragraph, near-verbatim

'It is a static string in the HTML source — a .score-number CSS class rendering the percentage alongside the WIN count' — second sentence of the source-code paragraph; minor wording polish ('HTML source code' → 'HTML source') drops one word, no clause lost
'No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint produces this number' — verbatim
'the arithmetic is stated on the wins page but no script validates the count against the actual WIN registry' — verbatim modulo punctuation

'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator' — VERBATIM, preserved per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 (operator override: 'design cannot eat truth'); placed in ams-col-source paragraph 1, before the Section 6.6 link

'The denominator is chosen to include only N acknowledged falsifications while excluding unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries' — preserved in the section's variant paragraph (ams-variant), with the introductory 'When the model's own internal data is queried...' framing condensed to 'Internal queries return X depending on which data source...' — minor wording polish, full factual substrate preserved
content_preservation_audit.preservation_framing_note: Per curmudgeon H2: this audit uses 'near-verbatim (minor wording polish, no clause dropped)' rather than 'verbatim' to accurately characterize the diff. The substantive content-preservation claim stands; the framing now matches the actual edit precision.
preserved_internal_anchors: #p3-failures (hero sc-sublabel Details link — retained; section also links to it)
#part6 onclick=showTab('predictions') (Section 6.6 full source-code analysis — retained in section's source-code paragraph)

#timestamp-error onclick=showTab('timestamp') (NEW cross-reference added by the section's ams-cross paragraph — strengthens the content relationship between the hardcoded-number critique and the Timestamp Error tab's status_history.json analysis; this cross-ref was implicit in the old prose, now explicit). The added anchor is also called out in stronger_arguments[0] per curmudgeon's recommendation.
content_preservation_audit.removed_prose_items_note: No prose lost vs. v1. The 'self-serving denominator' sentence remains preserved verbatim per the operator override that landed during v1 revision.

change_4_css_rules_for_accuracy_math_sec.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js inside the const CSS template literal block, near the existing .sc-card / .sc-sm rules (~line 520 area).
change_4_css_rules_for_accuracy_math_sec.h3_styling_rationale: The visible <h3> is styled to LOOK like a small-caps eyebrow (.78rem→.82rem uppercase, letter-spaced, sans, --ink-3) so it visually reads as section metadata, not as a competing chrome heading. But it IS a real <h3> element semantically — so screen-reader heading-navigation, document-outline tools, and any third-party indexer pick it up. We get visual restraint AND a11y reach. The h3-as-styled-eyebrow pattern is established precedent in editorial design (see e.g. The New York Times article-section headers).
change_4_css_rules_for_accuracy_math_sec.toc_consideration: The new <h3> WILL be picked up by any TOC generator that scans for h2/h3 inside #overview. This is acceptable: 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' is a real navigable subsection, and a TOC entry pointing to it is correct behavior. If the team prefers to suppress this specific h3 from the TOC, the existing TOC scanner can be filtered to ignore .ams-heading via a class-based skip mechanism (one-line addition). Acceptance criterion #11 verifies the TOC behavior is whatever the team chooses (either present-as-entry or filtered-out — the test fails on accidental change).
token_dependencies: --ink-3 (EXP-202) — heading + variant + cross metadata. Dark: #8a8680. Light: #6E675B.

--rule (EXP-202) — border-left. Dark: #2e2e4a. Light: #E4DDCB. Note: --rule has a known WCAG fail (1.30:1 dark, 1.35:1 light per c_2 finding) but it is used here as a decorative border, not as text — so it does not need to meet text contrast minimums. Acceptance criterion #14 makes this explicit.
--card-bg (existing) — NOT used here (the section sits on page bg, not card)
change_4_css_rules_for_accuracy_math_sec.font_family_note: The section inherits body font-family from the <main> cascade (default --serif after EXP-207 integrates; system-sans fallback otherwise). Because .ams-p is prose, it renders in --serif post-A1; .ams-heading is chrome and explicitly sets --sans; <code> sets --mono. This means EXP-211-redesign-v2 works even before EXP-207 has integrated (system-sans fallback usable but unpolished). The role distinction only becomes visible after Phase A1 lands.

change_5_no_changes_to: The Silent Failures static-template sc-card in sections.json part1.html — unchanged.
test.js existing assertions — untouched; this EXP adds new assertions (listed in acceptance_criteria).
Kill Shots tab / button / heading styles — untouched (NC3).
Latest Findings breaking-news block (EXP-212) — untouched (NC4 preserved).
Typography application (EXP-207) — untouched; v2 consumes its tokens but introduces no new font-family literals.
Verdict legend block — untouched (sits between Downloads and Latest Findings).

acceptance_criteria: AC1 — hero sublabel arithmetic stripped: grep -c "Honest accuracy:" docs/index.html returns 0 inside the 'Actual Failures' sc-card sublabel (the live aside copy reframes the phrase as 'Honest accuracy ${honestAccuracy}, not the headline...' inside ams-col-arith).
AC2 — bordered accent box deleted: grep returns 0 occurrences of 'border:2px solid var(--accent)' inside the Overview tab region (#overview).
AC3 — single section element inserted: grep -c 'class="accuracy-math-section"' docs/index.html returns exactly 1.

AC4 — semantic correctness: the section element is <section role="region" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">, NOT <aside>. test.js assertion: HTML contains the exact substring '<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">' AND does NOT contain 'class="accuracy-math-aside"'.

AC5 — visible heading present: HTML contains the exact substring '

Accuracy math — supporting detail

'.

AC6 — defensive rendering: when variantSources is empty (forced via test fixture or empty config), docs/index.html does NOT contain the substring 'Internal queries return depending' (note double space) and does NOT contain an empty

tag. The ams-variant paragraph is conditionally absent.

AC7 — internal anchors preserved: grep counts for #p3-failures, #part6, and #timestamp-error increase or stay flat versus pre-integration baseline (none drop). #timestamp-error MAY increase by 1 (the section adds a new cross-reference to the Timestamp Error tab — the only new internal anchor this EXP introduces).

AC8 — placement (one-scroll reachability), 1080p desktop: Playwright headless Chromium at 1920×1080 viewport, navigated to /docs/index.html#overview. DOM.getBoundingClientRect() on .accuracy-math-section .ams-heading returns top <= 1080 (above first-viewport fold WITHOUT any user scroll). The first arithmetic line (.ams-p inside .ams-col-arith) returns top <= 1080 OR top <= 1.4 * 1080 = 1512 (reachable within at most one half-scroll). Concrete measured value at integration time, not aspirational.
AC9 — placement, 1440p desktop: Playwright at 2560×1440. .accuracy-math-section .ams-heading top <= 1440 (above first-viewport fold). First arithmetic line top <= 1440 (full above-fold).

AC10 — placement, mobile (390×844, iPhone 14 baseline): Playwright at 390×844. .accuracy-math-section .ams-heading top <= 844 OR <= 1.5 * 844 = 1266 (reachable within first viewport scroll). Single-column ams-grid layout verified at this width (grid-template-columns resolves to '1fr' per the @media(max-width:720px) rule).

AC11 — TOC behavior consistent: count of <a href="#accuracy-math-heading"> in the rendered TOC is the team-decided value (default: 1 entry for the new h3). test.js asserts the chosen count; any unintended change fails the test.

AC12 — accessibility, programmatic name: the <section role="region"> element's aria-labelledby resolves to the h3's text content via getAccessibleName(). test.js assertion uses Playwright accessibility snapshot to verify the section's accessible name is exactly 'Accuracy math — supporting detail'.

AC13 — accessibility, NOT a complementary landmark: HTML contains no <aside class="accuracy-math-aside"> element (the v1 landmark-skip element is gone). test.js asserts. This is the primary fix for operator finding #2.

AC14 — WCAG AA contrast: --ink-2 on --bg measures 8.33:1 dark-theme (#b8b5ad on #1a1a2e) and 12.16:1 light-theme (#3A352D on #fff) — both PASS AA body text 4.5:1 with margin. --ink-3 on --bg measures 4.71:1 dark-theme (#8a8680 on #1a1a2e) and 5.59:1 light-theme (#6E675B on #fff) — both PASS AA body text 4.5:1 (dark-theme passes by 0.21, tight; light-theme passes comfortably). All four ratios computed via standard WCAG 2.1 relative-luminance formula (sRGB → linear → L = 0.2126*R + 0.7152*G + 0.0722*B; contrast = (L1+0.05)/(L2+0.05)). The section uses --ink-2 for body and --ink-3 for the h3 + variant + cross metadata, so all text in the section passes AA in both themes.

AC15 — --rule decorative use only: the 3px solid var(--rule) left border is decorative (not text), so it does NOT need to meet WCAG text contrast. The known c_2 finding (--rule contrast 1.30:1 dark / 1.35:1 light vs 3:1 required) does not apply to text-bearing surfaces in this section. test.js verifies the section's text characters never have --rule as their computed color.

AC16 — print pathway: node build.js pdf produces a PDF where the section's text renders in the print --ink-2 (#3A352D) over white — measured contrast 12.16:1, passes AA. The 3px var(--rule) border-left renders as a light-grey rule (#E4DDCB) on paper, visible but unobtrusive. The grid collapses to single-column in print (existing @media print rules apply).
AC17 — build reproducibility: fresh clone + node build.js html produces byte-identical docs/index.html (modulo embedded date line). NC6 honored.

AC18 — visual regression, Overview structural order: the post-integration Overview tab DOM order is: title-block → verdict-bar-chart (EXP-209) → sc-hero scorecard → accuracy-math-section (NEW) → Downloads heading → verdict-legend → breaking-news (Latest Findings, EXP-212 position) → nav.toc. Confirmed via Playwright DOM snapshot at integration time.

AC19 — three new test.js assertions added (collapsing AC4/AC5/AC13 into testable form): (a) docs/index.html contains exactly one <section ... class="accuracy-math-section" ...> element; (b) docs/index.html does NOT contain class="accuracy-math-aside" (the v1 element name is gone); (c) the Overview tab region contains no <div> with inline style starting 'border:2px solid var(--accent)'. Existing assertions unchanged.
AC20 — kicker phrasing fixed: the <h3 class="ams-heading"> text is exactly 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' (curmudgeon H3 fix). test.js asserts.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why <section role="region"> instead of <section> alone? <section> on its own is announced by screen-readers as 'region' only when it has an accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby). The redundant role="region" is belt-and-suspenders.
anticipated_objections.response: Belt-and-suspenders is intentional. The behavior of <section> without a name varies across screen-reader / browser combos: in some, an unnamed <section> is announced; in others, it is silently treated as a generic <div>. With aria-labelledby pointing to a real visible <h3>, the <section> WILL have an accessible name and SHOULD be announced. Adding role="region" forces the announcement consistently across user agents. The redundancy costs zero bytes (an attribute) and prevents one class of cross-browser a11y bug. WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices recommends the explicit role="region" + aria-labelledby pattern for exactly this case (a 'region' that supports the main content and warrants its own landmark).

anticipated_objections.objection: Promoting the kicker to <h3> adds a TOC entry. The Overview's TOC was deliberately curated; adding 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' to it is scope creep.
anticipated_objections.response: Acceptable tradeoff. The h3 is a real navigable subsection — having it appear in the TOC is correct behavior, not bug. If the team prefers to suppress it, the existing TOC scanner can filter out .ams-heading in a one-line addition (acceptance criterion #11 documents the team's choice and locks it via a test). The trade is: visible TOC entry (small addition) for full a11y reach (the load-bearing critique becomes navigable to screen-reader users). v1's 'styled <div> kicker outside TOC' was the wrong tradeoff for a critique that screen-reader users could miss entirely.

anticipated_objections.objection: The section now has TWO independent screen-reader pathways (landmark navigation via region + heading navigation via h3). Is this overkill for a supporting-detail surface?
anticipated_objections.response: Not overkill — both pathways exist organically and require zero extra annotation effort. Landmark navigation comes free with <section role="region" aria-labelledby>. Heading navigation comes free with <h3>. The same markup serves both. The cost is one extra HTML attribute (role="region") plus changing a styled <div> to a styled <h3>. The benefit is the load-bearing critique is reachable from EITHER navigation mode, and screen-reader users with different navigation preferences (some prefer landmarks, others prefer headings) all reach the critique. The 'overkill' framing inverts the cost-benefit — single-pathway markup leaves users with the wrong preferred mode hunting for the content.

anticipated_objections.objection: 1080p one-scroll reachability is fragile. If a future EXP adds content above the section (e.g., a new hero element, a new banner), the section drops below the fold again.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct, and the right fix is forward-looking integrity: AC8/AC9/AC10 are not one-time integration checks but PERSISTENT test.js assertions running on every build. Any future EXP that pushes the .accuracy-math-section .ams-heading top below the viewport fail-threshold breaks the build. Reachability is now a contract, not a one-time check. This is consistent with HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-MODE-001's broader direction: 'reachability and a11y are tested invariants, not snapshot-time observations.'

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not use option (a) — keep accuracy math inline in the hero stat-card sublabel, just demoted via typography? That's the simplest fix for one-scroll reachability.
anticipated_objections.response: Considered and rejected. Option (a) re-inflates the hero scorecard's third card with the arithmetic clause, which is exactly what the original brief said to demote. The brief's goal is consolidation: ONE accuracy-math surface, not two (hero sublabel + bordered box) and not zero (deleted entirely). Option (b) — repositioned section above-fold — preserves consolidation AND reachability. The section's scrollY is measured (AC8/9/10) at integration time and gated by a test, so the reachability claim is not aspirational. Option (a) would also re-introduce the chroma issue (the third sc-card has a red border-left that EXP-210 neutralizes; an inline arithmetic re-introduction means the demotion competes for visual pitch with the red chroma every time EXP-210 lands or revises).

anticipated_objections.objection: The defensive ternary ${accuracyVariantList ? ... : ''} adds template-literal complexity. A future maintainer reading the source might miss the conditional and 'fix' the apparent bug by removing the ternary.
anticipated_objections.response: Mitigated three ways: (1) the ternary is now INLINE in change_3.new_section_html (curmudgeon H1 fix) — a maintainer reading the integration target sees the ternary in context, not in a separate spec section; (2) AC6 is a regression test that fails the build if the ternary is removed and variantSources is empty; (3) a code comment immediately preceding the ams-variant paragraph documents the intent. Total cost: one comment + one test. The previous v1 arrangement (ternary documented only in anticipated_objections[5], not in the integration target) was the actual fragile state.

anticipated_objections.objection: What about Phase A1 ordering? v2 still depends on EXP-202 tokens (--ink-2, --ink-3, --rule, --sans, --mono).
anticipated_objections.response: Same as v1: Phase A1 (EXP-202/207/208/209/210/212/213) is integrated per status.json. Token dependencies are satisfied. v2 inherits the dependency without change. If somehow A1 unintegrates (force-rollback, etc.), v2 still renders with system-sans fallback and browser-default grey ink — usable but unpolished. AC4/AC5 do not depend on token availability. AC14 contrast measurements assume A1 tokens; if those tokens shift values in a future EXP-202 revision, AC14's stated ratios must be re-measured.

anticipated_objections.objection: Adding AC8/AC9/AC10 as Playwright-based tests increases CI runtime and adds a new dependency surface. Worth it for a design-refresh EXP?
anticipated_objections.response: Worth it specifically because the operator's finding #3 explicitly directed 'include a concrete viewport check in acceptance_criteria'. Narrative reachability claims have already failed once (v1 hedged 1-2 scrolls, ended up two-scroll on 1080p). Concrete measurement is the operator-mandated bar. If the team prefers a lighter check than full Playwright (e.g., a static layout-budget assertion: section appears within first 1500 vertical pixels of the Overview tab content), AC8/9/10 can be downgraded to that — but the principle of measured-not-narrated stands. v2 specifies Playwright as the conservative default; the team can negotiate the implementation.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: 4+4 failure accounting is calibration content
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Explicitly preserved AND now reachability-gated. The hero 4+4 Actual Failures sc-card keeps its sc-number, sc-label, and the He-admits/We-found narrative. The arithmetic moves to the section directly below (one-scroll reachable, AC8/9/10 measured). The Details link (#p3-failures) is kept on the hero card AND added to the section.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Latest Findings is evidence, not ticker chrome
Dark theme is differentiator, not oversight
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: AC14 measures both dark and light theme contrasts for both --ink-2 and --ink-3, all four ratios stated. Dark theme primary verified.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Hero thesis candidate is a CANDIDATE
Computed-counts rule (NC5)
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: All numeric values in the section are derived from generate-html.js context variables (${counts.total}, ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}, ${silentFailures}, ${honestAccuracy}, ${failures.dome_claimed_accuracy}, ${accuracyVariantList}). No hardcoded numbers.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Dark theme stays
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: All tokens consumed have dark-theme overrides; AC14 measures dark-theme contrasts.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Latest Findings stays on overview
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Not touched (EXP-212 already integrated; section sits before Downloads, Latest Findings remains in its post-EXP-212 position between verdict-legend close and nav.toc open).

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Computed counts rule
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: All values derived; no hardcoded numbers in section.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Build reproducibility
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Static template literal edits + CSS additions only.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Tests green
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: AC19 lists three new test.js assertions; AC8/9/10 add Playwright reachability tests; AC14 stated contrast ratios; AC6 defensive-rendering test. Existing assertions unchanged.

preserved_load_bearing_content: Hero scorecard 4+4 Actual Failures sc-card: sc-number, sc-label, He-admits/We-found narrative, and the #p3-failures Details link — all retained verbatim.
Honest accuracy percentage (${honestAccuracy}) — relocated to section, preserved.
Accuracy variant list (${accuracyVariantList}) — relocated to section's ams-variant paragraph, conditionally rendered.
Source-code critique clauses — all preserved near-verbatim with one minor wording polish ('HTML source code' → 'HTML source'). The 'self-serving denominator' sentence preserved verbatim.
Internal anchor #p3-failures — retained on hero card AND added to section.
Internal anchor #timestamp-error — newly added cross-reference, called out as an argumentative gain (curmudgeon stronger_arguments[0]).
Silent Failures static-template sc-card in sections.json — not touched.
All generate-html.js computed context variables (accuracyVariantList, accuracyVariantDetail, honestAccuracy) — unchanged; only DOM placement of the rendered values changes.
out_of_scope_for_this_exp: Red-palette neutralization on the hero 4+4 sc-card border-left (#c45050 → var(--rule)) — EXP-210 territory (already integrated per status.json).
Silent Failures static template harmonization — follow-up issue if needed.
Pie-chart SVG var() fills — Phase B1 (HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001).
Hero thesis sentence — EXP-A3 / EXP-208 scope.
Universal related-reading footer per tab — Phase B+ scope.
Hero scorecard layout redesign (grid-template-columns, card proportions) — not touched.
notes_for_human_review_group: ELEMENT CHOICE: <section role="region" aria-labelledby> with visible <h3>. Selected from operator option (b) for finding #2. Alternative option (a) — <div> with heading — was considered but <section> with explicit role is more semantically correct (the content IS a labeled region of related content, not a generic block).

TOC ENTRY: The new <h3> will be picked up by the TOC scanner by default, adding 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' as a TOC entry. If the team prefers to suppress this entry, AC11 makes the choice explicit and lockable via a test. Analyst recommends LEAVING it in TOC — it's a real navigable subsection.

REACHABILITY GATE: AC8/9/10 use Playwright-based DOM measurements. If the team's CI doesn't currently run Playwright-based assertions on docs/index.html, this EXP may need a small CI infrastructure addition — flag for review. Alternative: lighter static-layout assertion (section appears within first N vertical pixels of Overview content). Analyst's default is Playwright (per operator's 'concrete viewport check' direction); team may downgrade.

CONTRAST RATIOS: AC14 states measured ratios for --ink-2 and --ink-3 in both themes. All four pass AA body 4.5:1, but --ink-3 dark-theme is tight (4.71:1, passes by 0.21). If a future EXP-202 revision shifts --ink-3 darker, AC14 would need re-measurement. Flag for monitoring.

HEADING TEXT CHOICE: 'Accuracy math — supporting detail' (curmudgeon H3 fix). The v1 wording 'Accuracy math — detail for the calibrated reader' was self-flagged as editorializing about audience. The new wording is purely descriptive.

CC2 PROMOTED FROM CC TO ACCEPTANCE GATE: One-scroll reachability moved from a counter-consideration narrative claim to AC8/9/10 measured assertions. This is the structural lesson from operator finding #3 — 'reachable within 1-2 scrolls' is squishy; measured scrollY targets are firm.

DEFENSIVE TERNARY INLINE: Per curmudgeon H1, the ${accuracyVariantList ? ... : ''} ternary is now in change_3.new_section_html itself rather than buried in anticipated_objections. AC6 is the regression test.

CLASS RENAME ama- → ams-: The CSS prefix changes to match the section element. If integrity tests regex-match on the old 'ama-' prefix, those will need updating; analyst checked and found no existing assertions on the prefix (search of test.js + monitor/integrity/). Flag if found post-integration.

NEW CROSS-REFERENCE PRESERVED: The #timestamp-error cross-reference added in v1 is preserved in v2. Curmudgeon stronger_arguments[0] noted this as an argumentative gain; v2 calls it out explicitly in preserved_load_bearing_content.

INTEGRATION ORDER: v2 supersedes v1. If v1 was already partially integrated (it was not — v1 was DRAFT_AWAITING and never shipped), the decider should treat v2 as the canonical Phase A2 content and ignore v1. If decider has integrated any part of v1 by accident, AC1/AC2/AC3 detect the v1 element (.accuracy-math-aside class) and fail the build.

CURMUDGEON RE-REVIEW: Per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001, this v2 routes back through curmudgeon under the same conditional-approval rubric. If curmudgeon clears with no new ESCALATE findings, integration is automatic.

ISS-1425 CLOSURE: Already closed (status: wontfix, closed 2026-04-25T09:25:39Z). v2's purpose is to land the revision the closure was contingent on. No re-open of ISS-1425 needed; the closure note already references this revision.

AC8/9/10 placement measurements at three viewport sizes (1080p, 1440p, 390×844) with explicit scrollY thresholds.

integration_hold.blocked_until: Conditional integration per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001 — once curmudgeon clears v2 (no new ESCALATE findings), decider may integrate without a fresh operator HNOTE.

(b) Phase A1 tokens still integrated (EXP-202 — verified per status.json patches_applied tracking)
(c) AC1–AC20 acceptance criteria all pass at integration time
integration_hold.reason: Per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001 step 4: 'No further operator HNOTE needed if curmudgeon re-review is clean.' This v2 ships under the conditional-approval pathway.

completion_signal.action: Signal to the decider: 'Phase A2 v2 redraft complete. Addresses operator findings #2 #3 and additional --ink-3 contrast requirement, plus curmudgeon minor edits H1–H4. Route to curmudgeon for re-review under the conditional-approval rubric. If curmudgeon clears with no new ESCALATE findings, integration is automatic per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001 step 4.'

close_on_fix.resolution: ISS-1425 already closed. No issue to close on this revision; the closure note references the v2 revision as the revision-pending content.

completion_signal.curmudgeon_request_notes: v2 of EXP-211-redesign — adversarial re-review under HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001 rubric. Probes specific to v2: (a) does <section role="region" aria-labelledby> + visible <h3> actually fix the landmark-skip a11y regression? Verify via Playwright accessibility-tree snapshot (section is announced as 'region' not 'complementary'; h3 enters document outline). (b) AC8/9/10 reachability measurements — run Playwright at all three viewports and confirm scrollY values match the spec. (c) AC14 contrast measurements — verify the stated ratios via independent contrast calculator (e.g., WebAIM Contrast Checker) at the actual --ink-2/--ink-3/--bg token values from EXP-202 (dark and light themes). (d) AC6 defensive-rendering test — verify the inline ternary in change_3.new_section_html is correctly transcribed and the test fixture forces an empty variantSources case. (e) content preservation — diff the v2 section prose against v1 and against the original bordered-box prose; confirm no regression on the 'self-serving denominator' sentence and no new prose loss. (f) NC5 computed-counts rule — grep the section HTML for any hardcoded numbers or percentages outside template substitutions. (g) class rename ama-* → ams-* — confirm no broken CSS selectors and no integrity-test regex that still expects the old prefix. (h) TOC entry behavior — verify the team-decided h3 inclusion / suppression matches AC11. (i) AC18 Overview structural order — verify the post-integration DOM order matches the spec. Apply the same rubric as v1 review (queue_id 240). Recommended_action expectations: minor_edit if any new findings surface, close if clean.

tldr_evidence: Phase A2 v2 redraft of EXP-211 per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001. v2 fixes the three operator findings flagged in HNOTE-ISS-1425-OPERATOR-SIDES-WITH-CURMUDGEON-001: (1) <aside> changed to <section role="region" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading"> with a visible <h3 class="ams-heading"> — eliminates landmark-skip a11y regression and adds heading-navigation pathway; (2) section placement documented correctly at 'after sc-hero close, before Downloads' with AC8/9/10 measured Playwright reachability tests at 1080p, 1440p, and 390×844 mobile — concrete scrollY thresholds replace v1's narrative '1-2 scrolls' claim; (3) AC14 states measured WCAG contrast ratios for --ink-2 and --ink-3 over --bg in both themes (all four ratios pass AA body 4.5:1). Plus four curmudgeon minor edits (defensive ternary inlined, 'verbatim' softened, kicker text changed, CC2 promoted to measured AC).
tldr_verdict: DRAFT v2 — ready for curmudgeon re-review under conditional-approval rubric. No verdict change, no claim change, no content loss vs v1. The 'self-serving denominator' sentence preserved verbatim (operator override from HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 honored). All v1 internal anchors preserved; #timestamp-error cross-reference (introduced in v1) preserved. If curmudgeon clears v2 with no new ESCALATE findings, the decider may integrate without a fresh operator HNOTE per HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001 step 4.
